title: DSDS Example Definition
description: A unified example model used across DSDS. Each example provides a visual or interactive demonstration via a presentation, a literal value, or both. Optional title, description, and value properties provide context. Presentations are independent typed objects defined in presentation.schema.json for images, videos, code snippets, and URLs. The `example` def is used as an `example[]` array within other guideline items (best-practice examples, variant previews, state previews, interaction illustrations, anatomy previews, motion examples, content label examples, and localization examples).

example.description: A single example. Every example requires at least a presentation or a value (or both). The presentation provides a visual or interactive demonstration. The value carries a literal value for API property examples. The optional title and description provide context. When only a value is provided without a presentation, the example represents a concrete data point (e.g., a prop value of 'primary' or a token value of 44) — tools SHOULD render these as table rows or inline displays rather than visual demonstrations.

title.description: A human-readable caption or title for the example. This is a display label (e.g., 'Primary button in default state'). For API property examples, use `value` for the literal content and `title` for the human-readable label.

description.description: An explanation of the example — why it is encouraged, discouraged, or notable.

value.description: A literal value carried by the example. Used for API property examples where the content is a concrete value rather than a visual demonstration. Accepts any JSON type: string (e.g., 'primary'), number (e.g., 44), boolean (e.g., true), object, array, or null. When provided without a presentation, this value alone constitutes the example. Do not use `value` for display text — use `title` for captions and `description` for explanations.

presentation.description: The visual or interactive presentation for the example. Required when `value` is not provided. Optional when `value` is provided — value-only examples represent concrete data points (prop values, token values) that tools render as table rows or inline displays.
